summary: Implemented the Stage 7A non-live worktree and run-artifact layout guard slice.

tests_run.command: uv run --no-sync python -B -m pytest tests/test_worktree_artifacts.py -q -p no:cacheprovider

tests_run.summary: 273 passed plus Ruff, format, mypy, CLI smoke checks, file justification, public hygiene, planning integrity, skill inventory, source inventory, protected locks, and uv lock --check.

Worktree/run artifact layout derives det.evidence: build_worktree_run_layout derives worktrees/, runs/, artifacts/, and durable insights/ paths from safe task and worker-run identifiers; tests cover safe layout generation and unsafe identifier rejection.

Cleanup containment guards reject outsid.evidence: validate_cleanup_target resolves absolute or workspace-relative targets, rejects the workspace root itself, rejects outside targets, and rejects missing workspace roots before a deletion-capable caller can proceed.

Changed-file validation reports unsafe p.evidence: validate_changed_files reports unsafe changed files, unsafe allowed paths, and outside_allowed_paths violations using path strings only, so it can inspect diff summaries before Worker Result ingestion.

Focused tests cover safe layout generati.evidence: tests/test_worktree_artifacts.py covers deterministic layout, traversal/drive/slash rejection, cleanup root/outside/missing-root guards, and allowed-path validation.

risks: Stage 7A is deliberately non-live: it does not create git worktrees, remove directories, invoke Codex, or wire the layout into CodexExecBackend yet.
The next Stage 7 slice should connect these primitives to worker launch request construction and diff-summary capture through injected runners before any real cleanup command is enabled.
follow_up_tasks: Shape Stage 7B to connect WorktreeRunLayout to WorkerLaunchRequest construction and planning worker-run metadata.
Shape a later guarded cleanup slice that builds and tests git worktree prune/remove command plans without deleting outside configured workspace roots.
handoff_notes: Stage 7A adds pure path and guard primitives only. Future work should reuse worktree_artifacts.py instead of re-implementing path layout or cleanup containment in backend code.
